source_lists.title: Manic Pixie Dream Girl
source_lists.desc:
This term was coined by writer Nathan Rabin.

My interpretation of the trope is: A man going through a personal crisis meets a wacky, free-sprited, quirky girl who "saves" him. 

In his review for the film "Elizabethtown" (2005), Nathan Rabin of The A.V. Club created the term "Manic Pixie Dream Girl" to describe the "bubbly, shallow cinematic creature" stock character type that he stated Kirsten Dunst plays in the film.

Manic Pixie Dream Girl on Wikipedia
Manic Pixie Dream Girl on TV Tropes
Nathan Rabin apologizes for creating Manic Pixie Dream Girl

There are Manic Pixie Dream Men/Boys too: Titanic, Benny & Joon, I Love You Man, No Reservations, ( I removed Elf)

More characters: "Dharma & Greg", Phoebe Buffay on "Friends",… more

source_lists.title: Manic Pixie Dream Girl
source_lists.desc: The Manic Pixie Dream Girl (MPDG) is a stock character type in films. Film critic Nathan Rabin, who coined the term after seeing Kirsten Dunst in Elizabethtown (2005), describes the MPDG as "that bubbly, shallow cinematic creature that exists solely in the fevered imaginations of sensitive writer-directors to teach broodingly soulful young men to embrace life and its infinite mysteries and adventures". MPDGs are said to help their men without pursuing their own happiness, and such characters never grow up; thus, their men never grow up. (from Wikipedia)

source_lists.title: — manic pixie dream girl
source_lists.desc: oh look at me! i'm so quirky and different, I'm a shining bubble of light and glitter and I'll fill your life with marvelous joy!

source_lists.title: Manic Pixie Dream Girl
source_lists.desc:
I'm sure everybody has heard of this term by now, but I figured I'd compile a list (I'm sure there's many out there that already exist).

Suggestions welcome!

source_lists.title: Manic Pixie Dream Girl
source_lists.desc: A Manic Pixie Dream Girl or MPDG, is a term coined by film critic Nathan Rabin after seeing Elizabethtown. It refers to "that bubbly, shallow cinematic creature that exists solely in the fevered imaginations of sensitive writer-directors to teach broodingly soulful young men to embrace life and its infinite mysteries and adventures." A pretty, outgoing, whacky female romantic lead whose sole purpose is to help broody male characters lighten up and enjoy their lives.
